pattern: expect("compiled script cache shard mutex poisoned")
reason: Mutex poisoning is an internal process invariant; cache poisoning cannot be recovered as PHP-visible input.

pattern: expect("compiled script compile lock mutex poisoned")
reason: Compile lock poisoning means another Rust thread panicked while owning the lock.
Access-log lock poisoning is a host process failure, not request input.
Server startup cannot continue without a Tokio runtime.
Response builder invariants are controlled by validated status/header construction before this point.
Content-Length is formatted from an integer computed by the response body.
Response builder invariants are controlled by validated status/header construction before this point.
Static response builder uses validated local status and headers.
Builtin dispatch checks arity before calling module implementations.
Local control flow validates optional state before this invariant access.
Tests and module-local helpers use names from the registered builtin table.
bind_domain_path inserts the cloned domain key immediately before reading it back from the same map.
bind_domain_codeset inserts the cloned domain key immediately before reading it back from the same map.
The unpack helper validates byte width before converting to a fixed array.
The character candidate scanner only stores candidates with a first byte.
get_resources only reaches the type-error branch after matching a present first argument.
Mutex poisoning of the test override is a process failure, not PHP input.
Formatting into an in-memory Vec cannot fail under normal Rust formatting semantics.
Igbinary encodes in-memory byte slices and can only reach the u32 branch after exceeding u16; allocation limits prevent PHP-visible buffers larger than usize/u32 on supported CI targets.
Igbinary array headers encode an already allocated PhpArray length; supported targets cannot construct a PHP array requiring more than u32 entries here.
Base conversion emits only ASCII digit bytes before UTF-8 conversion.

pattern: expect("number_format decimals are capped for exact units")
reason: The number_format path caps precision before exact scale computation.
MessagePack string headers encode already allocated byte slices; supported targets cannot build a PHP-visible buffer requiring a length wider than u32 here.
MessagePack array headers encode an already allocated packed array length; supported targets cannot construct more than u32 entries here.
MessagePack map headers encode an already allocated PhpArray length; supported targets cannot construct more than u32 entries here.
The mysqli helper validates extension state before borrowing it.
Sodium keys are length-checked before fixed-array conversions.

pattern: expect("equal length arrays are not empty when a key is missing")
reason: Array comparison proves the equal-length non-empty condition before this access.
The row iterator branch only maps successful rows after query execution classification.

pattern: expect("global variable lvalue writes are supported")
reason: Global symbol writes use an lvalue kind that must support assignment.
The JIT array ABI validates packed array shape before fast length access.

pattern: expect("object storage re-borrowed across in-place property write")
reason: The temporary property value is removed before invoking the closure, so re-borrowing the same RefCell afterward is an object-storage invariant.
Frame target lookup validates bounds before indexing.

pattern: expect("fatal builtin diagnostic is appended above")
reason: Fatal builtin conversion appends the diagnostic immediately before reading it.
HTTP status has already been checked as present before response status conversion.
Dense bytecode dispatch pushes a frame before executing frame-local operations.
VM dispatch pushes a frame before executing frame-local operations.
Bytecode call return paths require the caller frame to remain active.
VM call/return paths require the caller frame to remain active.
Dense bytecode dispatch stores the active frame index before executing frame-local operations.
VM dispatch validates active frame state before frame-local mutation.
Fiber resume paths validate active suspension state before accessing the frame.
Generator resume paths validate active suspension state before accessing the frame.
Dense opcode decoding checks opcode family before conversion.
Dense opcode decoding checks opcode family before conversion.
Dense opcode decoding checks opcode family before conversion.
Argument parsing validates non-empty input before consuming the first item.
Argument parsing consumes an item immediately after a successful peek.
The query cursor walks within a Rust str boundary and advances by char length.
The helper validates argument count before extracting the only argument.

pattern: expect("array element lvalue can become a reference cell")
reason: Reference writes are limited to lvalue kinds that support reference cells.

pattern: expect("object property lvalue writes are supported")
reason: Object property assignment validates writable lvalue state before mutation.

pattern: expect("array element lvalue writes are supported")
reason: Array element assignment validates writable lvalue state before mutation.
call_user_func_array validates exact arity before popping callback and argument array values.

pattern: expect("call should be available before execution starts")
reason: Dense bytecode dispatch wraps the incoming call in Some before any unsupported early-return path can read it.

pattern: expect("call should be consumed exactly once")
reason: Dense bytecode dispatch consumes the call in exactly one successful execution branch after all early-return branches have exited.

pattern: expect("changed output is cloned before context drop")
reason: Output is cloned when a builtin changes output before dropping the borrowed builtin context.

pattern: expect("error output is cloned before context drop")
reason: Error output is cloned before dropping the borrowed builtin context for diagnostic conversion.
Static response builders use fixed statuses and validated headers.
Session IDs are formatted from hexadecimal ASCII bytes.

pattern: expect("static stream response builder is valid")
reason: Static stream responses use fixed statuses and validated headers.

pattern: expect("static empty response builder is valid")
reason: Static empty responses use fixed statuses and validated headers.

separate_for_write guarantees the storage Rc is unique before get_mut; a shared handle here is an engine bug, not PHP-visible input.
The in-place dim-write closure runs at most once per assignment, so the pending value is present on its only invocation.
The clone-based fallback only runs when the in-place closure never executed, so the pending assign value was not consumed.
The helper checks that the argument is present before reading the first argument.
The igbinary encoder currently treats lengths beyond the format helper width as unreachable for in-memory test and smoke fixtures.
The igbinary encoder currently treats array lengths beyond the format helper width as unreachable for in-memory test and smoke fixtures.
The MessagePack encoder currently treats lengths beyond the format helper width as unreachable for in-memory test and smoke fixtures.
The MessagePack encoder currently treats array lengths beyond the format helper width as unreachable for in-memory test and smoke fixtures.
The MessagePack encoder currently treats map lengths beyond the format helper width as unreachable for in-memory test and smoke fixtures.

pattern: expect("object storage re-borrowed across in-place property write")
reason: Object property writes complete before the same storage is reborrowed for the next mutation step.

pattern: expect("call should be available before execution starts")
reason: Dense-bytecode function dispatch keeps the original call value until an unsupported branch returns it.

pattern: expect("call should be consumed exactly once")
reason: Dense-bytecode function dispatch consumes the call value only on the selected execution branch.
Dense bytecode dispatch keeps the frame active while executing frame-local register operations.
chunks_exact(4) yields exactly four bytes per chunk, so the array conversion cannot fail.
The enclosing loop only runs while input.len() >= SNEFRU_BLOCK_SIZE, so the slice is exactly block sized.
chunks_exact(4) yields exactly four bytes per chunk, so the array conversion cannot fail.
The match arm only lists canonical names that encoding_rs_backend maps to a backend.

pattern: expect("validated env name and value contain no nulls")
reason: Environment names and values are rejected earlier in the loop if they contain nul bytes.

pattern: expect("static group name has no nul bytes")
reason: The group name is the static literal "nogroup", which contains no nul bytes.
xml_callback_is_enabled returns true only for present, enabled callback values, so the unwrap behind it cannot fail.
Indices below active_len are populated with Some before the (5..=8) length guard admits the superinstruction window.

pattern: expect("mixed index must point to a live slot")
reason: Mixed array indices are resolved through the live-entry index before value access or removal, so a selected slot must be populated.
The server cannot provide its configured execution model when an OS worker thread cannot be created, so startup terminates immediately.

pattern: expect("php worker dropped the request reply")
reason: A successfully queued FnOnce job must produce exactly one reply; loss means the worker or request job panicked and the result cannot be reconstructed.
The inline fallback invokes the reply-producing closure synchronously immediately before receiving its single result.
The enclosing function returns early unless session_state is Some a few lines above; the re-borrow cannot observe None on the same call.
The surrounding match arm enumerates exactly the value kinds opaque_value_tag maps; a None would mean the two lists diverged at compile evolution, not runtime input.
The owner slot is written Some(Box::new(...)) on the immediately preceding line; the deref cannot observe None.
A boundary is pushed whenever profiling opens a frame; pop only runs on the matching Some(boundary) close, so stack emptiness would be an engine bug, not request input.
